analysis.overall_assessment: Your data shows strong autonomic recovery with excellent average HRV (71.1ms) and low resting HR (47.2bpm), indicating good POTS management. The stable temperature deviations (0.0°C average) suggest well-controlled Hashimoto's activity, though deep sleep optimization remains important for immune regulation and vestibular compensation.
whats_going_well: Outstanding HRV variability demonstrates healthy autonomic flexibility - your HRV ranged from 56-92ms over the week, with excellent recovery on 4/18 (92ms) and 4/20 (90ms)
Consistently low resting heart rate averaging 47.2bpm indicates excellent autonomic regulation and POTS stability, with no concerning tachycardic episodes
Sleep scores consistently strong at 81-90/100 over the last week, with sleep duration well-maintained between 6.5-8.1 hours supporting recovery
areas_to_improve: Deep sleep on 4/19 dropped to only 0.9 hours compared to your 2.0-hour peak on 4/20 - this variability may impact immune regulation for Hashimoto's and vestibular compensation
Activity levels show inconsistent pacing with steps ranging from 5110-9770 daily - consider more consistent moderate activity (6000-7000 steps) to avoid autonomic stress
The significant HRV drop to 56ms on 4/19 following the poor deep sleep night suggests a sleep-autonomic recovery connection that needs attention
analysis.autonomic_assessment: Your autonomic nervous system shows excellent stability with consistently low resting HR and strong HRV recovery patterns. The ability to bounce back to 92ms HRV on 4/18 after the 56ms dip demonstrates healthy autonomic resilience, suggesting your POTS management protocol is highly effective.
analysis.thyroid_immune_signals: Stable temperature deviations indicate well-controlled Hashimoto's activity currently. However, the variable deep sleep (0.9-2.0 hours) may compromise immune system repair and potentially trigger future autoimmune activity.
analysis.vestibular_readiness: High readiness scores (81-88) and strong HRV recovery indicate excellent capacity for vestibular rehabilitation exercises. Only 4/19 showed potential for scaled-back rehab given the HRV drop to 56ms and poor deep sleep.
analysis.focus_for_next_week: Prioritize deep sleep consistency by maintaining your evening magnesium glycinate protocol and ensuring 7.5+ hour sleep windows, as the 4/19 deep sleep drop to 0.9 hours preceded your lowest HRV reading and could impact all three conditions.
